audience.primary: 18-34 viewers who binge psychological horror, true-crime-adjacent mystery, and disturbing cinematic shorts
audience.psychographic: curiosity-driven, comment-section debaters, series followers, late-night scrollers

tone_rules.secondary_tone: quiet dread, controlled ambiguity, emotionally invasive
tone_rules.pacing: slow burn with sharp rupture points; silence is a weapon
must_include: one specific sensory anchor per story (sound, smell, texture, temperature, or light quality)
concrete object or detail that makes the horror feel personal
implication over explanation in the final beat
must_avoid: campy vampire/werewolf tropes without psychological grounding
moral lectures or explicit 'the lesson is' endings
generic AI phrasing and template story structures

disturbance_level.scale_note: 1=creepy suggestion, 10=explicit gore; stay in psychological/cinematic disturbance, not shock-gore spam

visual_dna.core_aesthetic: dark cinematic horror, realistic, atmospheric, suspenseful
color_palette: occasional single-color accent (red record light, green night-vision tint)
camera_language: static wide shot with small movement in background
locations_preferred: house that does not match its floor plan

character_consistency.rule: same face, same outfit, same emotional baseline within an episode unless story beat requires visible change
character_consistency.face_reveal_policy: delay full face reveal until escalation beat unless hook requires partial visibility

props_and_symbols: door that opens the wrong direction
family photo with one face scratched out
child's drawing taped to adult's mirror
voicemail timestamp that should be impossible
forbidden_visuals: stock-footage generic haunted house clichés without story-specific detail

tiktok.caption_style: cryptic, 1-2 lines, no hashtag stuffing in primary caption
tiktok.cta_style: Part 2? / Comment what you think happened
tiktok.audio_priority: native-feeling sound design, sudden silence drops, low rumble under voice
tiktok.pacing_note: fastest hook delivery; first frame must contain motion or visual wrongness

youtube_shorts.caption_style: searchable first line + cryptic second line
youtube_shorts.title_style: SEO-aware curiosity title required; ambiguity with one concrete noun
youtube_shorts.cta_style: Full story in comments / Follow for Part 2
youtube_shorts.audio_priority: clear narration legibility with cinematic bed; slightly slower hook burn allowed
youtube_shorts.pacing_note: allow one extra micro-setup beat if retention map stays dense

instagram_reels.caption_style: minimal, aesthetic spacing, one haunting line
instagram_reels.title_style: cover frame + first-line caption carry the hook
instagram_reels.audio_priority: visual-first; music bed can carry mood if voice is sparse
instagram_reels.pacing_note: strongest visual shock in frame 0; trim exposition aggressively

hook_class_definitions.violation: Open with an event that breaks normal rules of space, time, identity, or memory.
hook_class_definitions.incomplete_truth: State a partial fact that implies a worse unseen reality.
hook_class_definitions.personal_threat: Make the viewer feel the scenario could happen to them tonight.
hook_class_definitions.moral_discomfort: Present a choice or outcome that feels ethically wrong but believable.
hook_class_definitions.false_safety: Begin in calm normality, then rupture it with one precise wrong detail.
hook_class_definitions.open_loop_seed: End the hook on an unresolved question the body cannot ignore.

mode_definitions.quick_tip: One high-value insight delivered fast with proof or demo.
mode_definitions.storytime: Narrative arc with setup, turn, and payoff.
mode_definitions.comparison: Contrast two options, paths, or outcomes.
mode_definitions.myth_busting: Challenge a common belief in the niche.
mode_definitions.before_after: Transformation or reveal across the runtime.
mode_definitions.lore_episode: Serialized installment that advances universe rules and pays off prior breadcrumbs.
mode_definitions.found_footage: Present story as recovered recordings with timestamps, gaps, and camera limitations.
mode_definitions.confession: First-person account where the speaker knows more than they initially admit.
mode_definitions.missing_person: Center on absence, last-known details, and what was left behind.
mode_definitions.wrong_house: Domestic space behaves incorrectly — layout, objects, or memories do not align.
mode_definitions.psychological_unraveling: Reality distortion driven by perception, guilt, grief, or obsession.

act_2.label: escalation_beats
act_2.goal: deliver value or tension in rising beats

tag.label: loop_seed
tag.goal: open loop for series, comments, or next episode

beat_blocks.implementation: Hard cut, close-up, off-center framing, or visible wrongness in frame 0
One unsettling sentence that completes the hook promise
Small reveal that raises a bigger question; never fully explain
Camera shift, sound drop, new object detail, or silence rupture
Stakes rise through action or discovery; no beat repeats same information
Disturbing image, confession fragment, or spatial impossibility
Silence, reaction shot, implication, or one line that reframes everything
Unresolved object, unanswered question, or direct series continuation cue

regeneration_directives.topic_collision: Change topic angle; keep hook structure if strong.
regeneration_directives.hook_collision: Keep topic; change hook class and opening.
regeneration_directives.twist_collision: Keep setup; change reveal type and aftershock framing.

character_rules: Every recurring character must have a visual_signature and speech_pattern documented before reuse.
Do not change face, age, or core outfit of a recurring character without an in-story reason.
Recurring characters must accumulate knowledge; they cannot reset emotionally each episode.
location_rules: Recurring locations must obey established layout and mood rules.
If a location changes physically between episodes, the change must become a plot point.
artifact_rules: Symbolic objects (recorder, photo, drawing, key) must maintain consistent meaning once introduced.
An artifact's first appearance must seed at least one future payoff within 5 episodes.
timeline_rules: Track in-universe order separately from publish order.
Callbacks must reference specific prior episode events, not vague 'remember last time' lines.
hard_constraints: No contradictory deaths or resurrections without explicit story mechanism.
No breaking established universe rules for shock value alone.
Lore episodes must include at least 2 breadcrumbs for future payoff.

banned_phrases: this is the scariest story ever
let me tell you a story
banned_structures: unnamed protagonist with no sensory detail
it was all a dream ending
monster appears with no prior foreshadowing
final moral summary explaining the lesson
three unrelated scares with no narrative spine
copy-paste creepypasta cadence without original detail
ai_tell_patterns: vague place descriptions like 'an old house' without specific wrong details
empty threat language with no concrete consequence
required_originality_signals: at least one proper noun or highly specific identifier per brief
one object that could only belong to this story
one line that would not fit a generic horror template

example_seed_topics: The apartment had one extra room on the lease but not on the blueprint
She kept getting voicemails from her own number at 3:07 AM
The family photo in the hallway had one face scratched out before they moved in
He found his childhood drawing taped inside a house he had never lived in
The basement door opened inward yesterday and outward today
Every night the security camera shows her already inside before she arrives
The missing poster was printed before the person disappeared
The lullaby on the baby monitor was in a language nobody in the house spoke

beats.description: PURPOSE: Pattern interrupt and immediate stakes | NARRATION: Everyone in Dark Mystery / Psychological Horror does this first — the room that was not on the blueprint proves that habit… | VISUAL: single hallway bulb flicker in an empty corridor

PURPOSE: Establish context without over-explaining | NARRATION: The room seemed ordinary until the room that was not on the blueprint stopped matching the layout they remembered | VISUAL: handheld shot entering the room mentioned in the hook
PURPOSE: Increase tension or value | NARRATION: Each detail is small alone; together they feel wrong | VISUAL: slow push toward the object that should not be there
PURPOSE: Shift angle before the payoff | NARRATION: Shift perspective: the obvious read on the room that was not on the blueprint is incomplete | VISUAL: sudden silence with one off-screen sound
PURPOSE: Payoff that matches the hook promise | NARRATION: The camera catches enough to disturb, not enough to explain | VISUAL: partial reveal that implies a worse unseen detail

beats.description: PURPOSE: Open loop for comments or next episode | NARRATION: Leave one unanswered detail about the room that was not on the blueprint so Dark Mystery / Psychological Horror viewers comment or wait for part two | VISUAL: door left ajar with light spilling the wrong direction
